mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand that lifts corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production indicates supply-side capacity utilization that supports short-term equity momentum during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in over 50 percent of recovery-phase observations, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark (Sharpe < 0.5) over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5 percent indicates extreme monetary accommodation that fuels liquidity-driven equity rallies, generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55 percent of low-rate observations, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for inflation expectations (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a stable or falling unemployment rate signals labor market tightening that compresses profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward 60-day returns in over 60 percent of cases, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating real GDP growth during late-cycle expansions triggers growth recession fears that suppress equity valuations, causing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55 percent of late-cycle deceleration periods, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52 percent in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in the index of industrial production reflect weakening manufacturing activity that immediately dampens short-term equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of declining INDPRO observations, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 1-year windows.
